title: Baasha Paarusad Version Lyrics Baasha
stanzas:
Baasha Paaru
baasha paaru
paal vadiyum mugatha paaru
pasuva pola gunatha paaru
pachcha raththam ozhugum podhu
pachcha kuzhandha sirippu paaru

peice piece-a kizhikkum podhu
yaesu pola poruma paaru
dhegamellaam vaegum podhum
kodi kodukkum vizhuthu paaradaa

ivan jaathagatha
maaththi vachcha
paavi yaaradaa

enrichedMetadata.faq:
Frequently Asked Questions

What is the meaning of the song?

The song reflects themes of strength and resilience, showcasing the character's journey and emotions.

In which movie does this song appear?

This song is from the movie Baasha, which is a cult classic in Tamil cinema.

Who is the singer of this song?

The song is sung by the legendary S. P. Balasubrahmanyam, known for his powerful vocals.

Who composed the music for this song?

The music for this song was composed by Deva, a prominent music director in Tamil films.

When was this song released?

This song was released on January 12, 1995.
enrichedMetadata.summary: The song Baasha Paarusad Version encapsulates the spirit of the protagonist in the movie Baasha, portraying a blend of nostalgia and energy. It conveys a sense of determination and strength, resonating deeply with listeners. The vibrant lyrics and S. P. Balasubrahmanyam's emotive singing evoke a powerful connection to the character's journey. This track remains a beloved classic, celebrated for its memorable melody and impactful delivery.
enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: Baasha Paarusad Version is a nostalgic melody that captures the essence of the iconic film Baasha. With S. P. Balasubrahmanyam's soulful voice and Deva's enchanting composition, this song resonates with fans of Tamil cinema. Experience the vibrant energy and heartfelt lyrics that make this track a timeless classic.
stanzaMeanings: Look at the king, look at the king, see the crown on his head, like a child who smiles when the green blood flows.
When a piece is broken, it feels like a loss, look at the body, everything will be fine when the flag is raised.
Who is the sinner who has changed his lineage?
In this mountain, has he thrown away the cloth?
